inventory_bar.missing_data_body: You haven’t found all the data yet. Return to the forest to continue your search.

video.subtitles:
WEBVTT

00:00:00.000 --> 00:00:02.600
Argh no... We’re in so much trouble.

00:00:02.600 --> 00:00:07.600
Hi I’m Luna, the manager of the market stalls here at Market Marshes.

00:00:07.600 --> 00:00:10.360
Our lives revolve around the Sasa River.

00:00:10.360 --> 00:00:14.480
But, as you can see, the river can flood our market without warning.

00:00:14.520 --> 00:00:17.800
We didn’t even have enough time to take the stalls down.

00:00:17.800 --> 00:00:22.360
We’ve been trying to predict flooding, but this weather forecast isn’t reliable enough.

00:00:22.360 --> 00:00:27.480
We’ve heard that AI can help to predict extreme weather events... like floods.

00:00:27.480 --> 00:00:29.840
With accurate early warning,

00:00:29.840 --> 00:00:32.720
we could protect our
market and avoid all this mess.

00:00:32.720 --> 00:00:35.160
So, can you do it?

learning_ticket.question: Welcome, I'm Professor Skye, your AI mentor! I'm here to guide you as you make crucial decisions about your AI.

success_response.text: First things first! To build your AI flood prediction tool, you need data that helps predict floods. Head into the forest and collect the data.

instructions.heading: Search the forest
instructions.description: The data you'll need to train a flood forecasting AI model is hidden in objects throughout the forest. Can you find and collect the missing data?

objective_reminder.body: Scroll through the forest to find the 12 objects containing the data you need.

has_enough_tokens.prompt: You have enough data to proceed to the next task, but collecting more data might help make it more accurate

learning_ticket.question: Good job! My system has flagged some possible errors in the data. Can you spot them?

easter_eggs.click_text: What, you've never met a talking lizard before?
The only thing I'm catching here is a cold!

instructions.heading: Select your data
instructions.description: From the data you found in the forest, pick the ones you think will be most helpful for generating an accurate prediction.

objective_reminder.body: From the data you found in the forest, pick the ones you think will be most helpful for predicting flooding.

learning_ticket_final.question: Incredible! You've trained your AI! 🎉 What do you want to do next?

answers.text: Let Luna know that our job here is done, and she can now use the AI to predict floods.
Test it first to see how accurate it is.

success_response.text: Good approach! We need to test the AI to make sure it will make accurate predictions before we let Luna use it.

failure_response.text: Before we let Luna use it, we need to test the AI to make sure it will make accurate predictions.

learning_ticket.question: Your AI will learn patterns from the data you chose. What happens if we missed some crucial data?

answers.text: It would cause more rain and worse flooding.
It would be harder for the AI to find useful patterns, leading to inaccurate flood forecasts.
AI is smart. It doesn't really matter what data we give it. It will figure it out.

success_response.text: Yeah, that’s right. Your choices impact the model’s accuracy.

failure_response.text: If we miss crucial details, it becomes much harder for the AI to find useful patterns, and our flood forecasts won't be accurate. This is why our human decisions are so important in designing AI effectively!

learning_ticket.question: Now to the main event - it's time to train the AI model! Watch as it learns from all that data, starting to spot patterns and make predictions.

failure_response.text: Not quite. AI models are only as accurate as the data we give them. Your data choices can't change how much it rains but they can impact how well the model predicts floods. Let's see how your model predicts floods.

instructions.heading: Clean the data
instructions.description: AI models rely on data being error-free to generate a reliable prediction. Let's clean the data we want to use.
instructions.interaction_slot_text: Tap the data that doesn't look right

objective_reminder.heading_description: Tap the data that doesn't look right.
objective_reminder.body: Look at the data and tap any that don’t look right to you.

learning_ticket.question: We have so much more data to clean! Can you teach Luna how to do it so she can do the rest?

answers.text: Look at the data to see if any values look unusual or are missing.
Call an AI agent to do this for you.

failure_response.text: Neither soap nor an AI agent will work for this. It's crucial for a human to examine the data themselves to catch anything that could mess up our predictions.

learning_ticket.question: In your next task, consider: which data sources would be most useful for predicting floods?
Now, we’ll use your data to train our AI model to make a flood forecast. This forecast will show how likely flooding is for a specific area at a specific time.
This is Luna's market at Market Marshes. Let’s start by looking at what a flood forecast for this area would look like.
Our forecast will be on the left. The market area is divided into sections to show which parts might flood. The forecast will use color to show flood risk.
Colors show the expected flood risk: Blue - no flood expected,  Yellow - mild flood expected, Red - severe flood expected.
What do you think this means for the market stalls in blue areas?

answers.text: Market stalls in the blue areas should be moved to a safer place.
Market stalls in the blue areas are safe where they are.

success_response.text: That's right! Stalls in the blue areas are safe for the time of this forecast.

failure_response.text: Not quite. Based on this forecast, stalls in blue areas are safe.

learning_ticket.question: Now that you know what a forecast looks like, let's train our AI model so it can create forecasts to help Luna and her community.
First, we give the AI model the input data you picked.

success_response.text: From the data, the AI starts learning. Its job is to find all the different patterns that floods can follow.

learning_ticket.question: Which data source should it use to learn patterns?

success_response.text: That's right! To identify accurate flood patterns, the AI needs to investigate and learn from different types of data.

failure_response.text: Not quite. To identify accurate patterns of floods, the AI needs to investigate and learn from different types of data.

learning_ticket.question: After learning patterns, the model makes flood predictions. For each set of conditions, it predicts when floods might happen and how severe they will be.
To check how well the model works, we'll test it on data from past floods that it hasn't seen before.

success_response.text: We kept these aside just for this moment!

learning_ticket.question: To truly test our AI, should we use data it's already seen during training?

success_response.text: Exactly! We test with new data to get a true picture of how well our AI can predict. Otherwise it may only work for data it already knows.

failure_response.text: Not quite. If the AI has already seen the data, it's like giving it the answers to a test. We need to see if it can figure out new problems before we use it in the real world.

learning_ticket.question: Move the slider to compare your model's predictions to what actually happened on those days. At the end, you'll see your model's final accuracy score.
You did it! Your model's accuracy score is 90%! This is the highest possible score, as no real-world forecast can ever be 100% certain. You're ready to go—let's generate a forecast!

Your model's accuracy score is in the 70-90% range, which is great! This means your model is accurate enough for real-world use. The maximum possible score is 90% because no forecast can ever be 100% certain. You can now move on to generating a forecast, or, if you'd like, you can try to improve it even more.
Your model’s accuracy score is under 70%, but you can improve it! Our goal is to reach an accuracy of 70-90%, and you can achieve that by going back and selecting a different combination of data.

Your AI model has successfully generated its very first flood forecast. And what a forecast! It indicates a high risk of flooding... in five days! This is our chance to get ahead of the storm. We need to alert Luna and her team right away so they can start preparing!

forecast.graph_warning: Warning: River expected to rise in 5 days
forecast.title: Your AI Prediction

instructions.heading: Save the market
instructions.description: Use your forecast to find the safest spots to set up the market stalls to avoid flooding.

objective_reminder.heading_description: Using your forecast, place the stalls in the safest areas of the market
objective_reminder.body: Note that normal stalls can only be placed where no flooding is expected (blue zones), while tall stalls are safe to place where mild floods are expected (yellow zones)

stalls_game.fail_message: Some market stalls aren’t positioned safely. Take a look at your forecast and try again.

luna_outro_video.subtitles:
WEBVTT

00:00:00.000 --> 00:00:02.840
Hello there. It’s so nice to see you again.

00:00:02.840 --> 00:00:04.080
Guess what?

00:00:04.080 --> 00:00:06.120
Your systems are working!

00:00:06.120 --> 00:00:08.720
The last time a flood came,
we had enough warning

00:00:08.720 --> 00:00:12.640
and were able to pack up the market before there was any damage.

00:00:12.640 --> 00:00:16.320
With more accurate predictions, we can stay prepared.

00:00:16.320 --> 00:00:19.120
Thanks so much for your help!
Bye for now.

real_world_video.subtitles:
WEBVTT

00:00:05.000 --> 00:00:07.000
Thanks, Professor Skye. Hey everyone, I'm

00:00:07.000 --> 00:00:09.000
Tal and I'm Grey. We work at Google

00:00:09.000 --> 00:00:10.000
Research on the flood forecasting team.

00:00:18.000 --> 00:00:20.000
Our team uses AI to predict where floods

00:00:20.000 --> 00:00:23.000
occur around the world so that we can get people warnings and help them stay

00:00:23.000 --> 00:00:25.000
safe. The quest you just took was

00:00:25.000 --> 00:00:27.000
inspired by our journey. What I love

00:00:27.000 --> 00:00:30.000
most about our work is that we're using science and technology to make a real

00:00:30.000 --> 00:00:34.000
difference in the lives of millions of people, especially in places where

00:00:34.000 --> 00:00:35.000
floods can be devastating.

00:00:35.000 --> 00:00:37.000
Floods are serious. They can destroy

00:00:37.000 --> 00:00:39.000
homes and cut off communities. But with

00:00:39.000 --> 00:00:43.000
the right data and the right tools, we can help reduce the impact and minimize

00:00:43.000 --> 00:00:45.000
damage. As a result, communities can

00:00:45.000 --> 00:00:47.000
recover faster after a flood.

00:00:47.000 --> 00:00:49.000
So, here's how it works. This should

00:00:49.000 --> 00:00:53.000
sound familiar because it's very similar to what you've just done during your

00:00:53.000 --> 00:00:55.000 line:65%
quest. First, we gather tons of publicly

00:00:55.000 --> 00:00:59.000 line:65%
available data. Things like rainfall, water levels, weather forecast, and

00:00:59.000 --> 00:01:01.000 line:65%
satellite images. We review the quality

00:01:01.000 --> 00:01:03.000
of the data and clean it if necessary.

00:01:03.000 --> 00:01:07.000
Then, we use AI to create detailed simulations of how water moves through

00:01:07.000 --> 00:01:09.000
the environment. Think of it like a

00:01:09.000 --> 00:01:10.000
virtual model of the real world.

00:01:10.000 --> 00:01:15.000
We train the AI model to predict when and where water will rise, how fast, and

00:01:15.000 --> 00:01:17.000
who may be affected. This kind of

00:01:17.000 --> 00:01:20.000
prediction is called flood modeling

00:01:20.000 --> 00:01:24.000
because even small changes in rainfall can completely change how flood behaves.

00:01:24.000 --> 00:01:25.000
Once our models are trained, we test

00:01:25.000 --> 00:01:28.000
them for accuracy. We then use our

00:01:28.000 --> 00:01:32.000
models to send early warnings, sometimes days in advance. These alerts show up in

00:01:32.000 --> 00:01:36.000
Google search, Google maps, and in some cases as notifications to your cell

00:01:36.000 --> 00:01:37.000
phone.

00:01:37.000 --> 00:01:38.000
We also work with governments and rescue

00:01:38.000 --> 00:01:42.000
team so they can act fast, help people on the ground, and get them out of

00:01:42.000 --> 00:01:45.000
danger in time. Our system covers

00:01:45.000 --> 00:01:46.000
floodprone areas in thousands of

00:01:46.000 --> 00:01:48.000
locations around the world

00:01:48.000 --> 00:01:52.000
and we are still working hard to expand to even more locations because we want

00:01:52.000 --> 00:01:54.000
flood forecasting to be available to

00:01:54.000 --> 00:01:55.000
everyone who needs it.

00:01:55.000 --> 00:01:57.000
It's important to remember that AI is a

00:01:57.000 --> 00:02:01.000
team effort. It involves many people from different backgrounds and different

00:02:01.000 --> 00:02:05.000
points of view. We have scientists like myself who are experts in floods and we

00:02:05.000 --> 00:02:07.000
work hand in hand with engineers like Tal

00:02:07.000 --> 00:02:09.000
who are experts in AI. We hope that this

00:02:09.000 --> 00:02:12.000
video helped you understand how everything you have done on the quest

00:02:12.000 --> 00:02:13.000
happens in real life.

00:02:13.000 --> 00:02:14.000
Back to you, Professor Sky.

learning_recap.question: Great job saving those market stalls, lets put together a quest report! Can you walk me through your process? What was your first step?

answers.text: Using the AI to predict floods

success_response.text: Thats right! You searched the forest for data points to learn about floods and train your model.

failure_response.text: Not quite, first you searched the forest and gathered relevant data!

learning_recap.question: What was the second step you took?

answers.text: Using the AI to predict floods

success_response.text: Right! You selected the most important data you found and used it to train your model.

failure_response.text: Not quite, you selected the most important data you found and used it to train your model.

answers.text: Using the AI to predict floods

success_response.text: That’s right! You used historical flood information to test your AI and make sure it is accurate enough

failure_response.text: Actually you used historical flood information to test your AI and make sure it is accurate enough

learning_recap.question: What was the final step you took?

answers.text: Using the AI to predict floods

success_response.text: You got it! You used your AI to predict where it would flood and placed the market stalls around it.

learning_recap.question: One final thing for our report - in this quest you used AI to help predict floods and provide early warning. What was the most important thing you learned about making AI helpful for a real-world problem like this?

answers.text: The AI can figure out everything by itself, so humans aren't really needed.
Humans can solve big problems like flood prediction perfectly on their own, without any AI.
Humans are essential for deciding what job the AI should do, designing it, collecting the right data, and using its output to make a real impact

success_response.text: This is a fantastic insight for our report! You've captured the most important idea: humans are essential for making AI useful. We decide what the AI does, what data it uses, and how to use it to solve real-world problems.

failure_response.text: AI's greatest power comes from human guidance. Humans are essential for deciding what the AI should do, giving it the right data, and using its predictions to solve real-world problems.

learning_recap.question: One last thing! This quest mirrored a real Google Research AI project where humans initiated and designed flood prediction tools for positive impact. You stepped into their shoes! Check out this video to hear from them.

tokens.name: Rainfall
tokens.token_collected_dialog: How much rain falls. More rain means more water in the river.

tokens.name: River Flow
tokens.token_collected_dialog: How deep and fast the water is flowing in the river.
tokens.eyebrow: You found the River Flow data!

tokens.name: Soil Moisture
tokens.token_collected_dialog: How much water the ground can soak up. If the ground is full, rain flows straight into the river.
tokens.eyebrow: You found the Soil Moisture data!

tokens.name: Satellite Imagery
tokens.token_collected_dialog: Pictures from space showing how far past floods reached. This shows exactly where the water went.
tokens.eyebrow: You found the Satellite Imagery data!

tokens.name: Temperature
tokens.token_collected_dialog: Temperature significantly affects processes like snowmelt and evaporation, directly impacting river water.

tokens.name: Evaporation
tokens.token_collected_dialog: How quickly water evaporates. This can dry out the ground, which might change how rain flows into the river.

tokens.name: Solar Radiation
tokens.token_collected_dialog: How much sun the land gets. More sun can make it hotter and dry things out, which might affect the river.
tokens.eyebrow: You found the Solar Radiation data!

tokens.name: Humidity
tokens.token_collected_dialog: When the air is really humid, less rain evaporates, meaning more water runs into the river.

tokens.name: Sorbet Sales
tokens.token_collected_dialog: A sweet little record that might reflect some weather conditions.
tokens.eyebrow: You found the Sorbet Sales data!

tokens.name: Town Gossip
tokens.token_collected_dialog: This is not directly related to the river, but people may mention things that are relevant.
tokens.eyebrow: You found the Town Gossip data!

tokens.name: Elevation
tokens.token_collected_dialog: How high or low the land is. Water flows downhill, so this can change how the river flows.

tokens.name: Vegetation
tokens.token_collected_dialog: Plants and trees can hold back water, which might affect how much goes into the river.
